variants.label: Session Plan
variants.optimizes_for: Upper-body calisthenics and conditioning with core stability emphasis; lumbar flexion avoided.

warmup.name: Bench-Supported Incline YTI
warmup.rationale: Restores T-spine extension and shoulder mobility, directly addressing postural restrictions driving lumbar overload.
warmup.sequencing_rationale: Opens the session to unlock the thoracic and shoulder mobility deficits that force lumbar compensation during upper-body work.

warmup.name: Kneeling Stability Ball Lat Stretch
warmup.rationale: Dynamic lat and upper-back stretch to further mobilise the shoulder complex and reduce upper-crossed syndrome tension.
warmup.sequencing_rationale: Follows YTI to deepen shoulder and lat mobility, preparing the upper body for loaded pulling and pressing patterns.

main.name: Band-Assisted Chin-Up (From Foot)
main.rationale: CNS-intensive vertical pull that builds upper-back and lat strength while band assistance respects 60% load tolerance.
main.sequencing_rationale: Placed first in main block while CNS is fresh; band support allows safe loading without lumbar compensation.

main.name: Push-Up to Knee-Drive
main.rationale: Horizontal push compound that integrates core anti-extension stability; knee-drive adds dynamic core demand without lumbar flexion.
main.sequencing_rationale: Follows vertical pull to balance upper-body pressing and pulling; knee-drive reinforces core stability without spinal load.

main.name: High Plank Bird Dog
main.rationale: Anti-rotation and core stability work that reinforces spinal stiffness and contralateral limb coordination without flexion stress.
main.sequencing_rationale: Placed after compounds to consolidate core control; bird dog pattern teaches anti-rotation stability essential for spinal protection.

main.name: Alternating Low Plank To Low Side Plank
main.rationale: Anti-lateral flexion and anti-extension work that builds oblique and core endurance without lumbar flexion or rotation.
main.sequencing_rationale: Follows bird dog to layer anti-lateral flexion stability; transitions core work toward conditioning density.

main.name: Burpee Broad Jump
main.rationale: Total-body plyometric conditioning that drives cardiovascular demand and upper-body power without loaded lumbar flexion.
main.sequencing_rationale: Placed after strength and core work to avoid pre-fatiguing prime movers; burpee format integrates upper-body and lower-body power.

main.name: Box Jump
main.rationale: Lower-body plyometric conditioning that builds power and metabolic demand while maintaining neutral spine throughout.
main.sequencing_rationale: Final conditioning block to finish session with high metabolic stimulus; box jump allows safe landing and neutral spine positioning.

cooldown.name: Ground Upper Trap Stretch
cooldown.rationale: Static stretch to release upper-trap tension and reinforce postural decompression after upper-body work.
cooldown.sequencing_rationale: Closes session with parasympathetic recovery; upper-trap release complements the T-spine mobility work from warmup.

plan.stimulus: Upper-body calisthenics and conditioning with core stability emphasis; lumbar flexion avoided.
plan.target_adaptation: Upper-body muscular endurance, cardiovascular capacity, and core anti-extension stability without lumbar overload.
plan.design_rationale: The member's mechanical low-back pain is driven by desk-induced hip flexor tightness and T-spine immobility, both of which amplify lumbar flexion and compressive load. This 45-minute session prioritises upper-body and conditioning work to avoid loaded lumbar flexion entirely, while opening with mobility drills (YTI, lat stretch) to restore T-spine extension and shoulder mobility—directly addressing the postural root cause. At 60% load tolerance, the session uses moderate-intensity calisthenics (band-assisted pull-ups, push-ups) and plyometric conditioning (burpees, box jumps) rather than heavy loaded movements. Core work is restricted to anti-extension and anti-rotation patterns (planks, bird dogs, Copenhagen holds) that spare the lumbar spine, and the session closes with static stretching to reinforce mobility gains and parasympathetic recovery.
plan.sequence_logic: The session opens with T-spine and shoulder mobility (YTI, lat stretch) to unlock the postural restrictions driving lumbar overload, then transitions into upper-body compound movements (band-assisted chin-ups, push-ups) while the CNS is fresh and the spine is primed. Core anti-extension work (planks, bird dogs) follows to reinforce spinal stability without flexion stress. Conditioning circuits (burpees, box jumps, running) are placed last to avoid pre-fatiguing the upper-body prime movers and to allow the session to finish with metabolic stimulus rather than strength fatigue. Static stretching closes the session to consolidate mobility gains and signal parasympathetic recovery.

provenance.prompt: Upper-body calisthenics or conditioning. Avoid loaded lumbar flexion.

decision_trace.name: resolve_prompt
decision_trace.detail: Extract concept terms from the coach's free-text prompt using the 3-pass resolver (exact → fuzzy → embedding).

inputs.prompt: Upper-body calisthenics or conditioning. Avoid loaded lumbar flexion.

decision_trace.name: load_constraints
decision_trace.detail: Load member equipment list and dislikes for downstream gates.

decision_trace.name: part_of_traversal
decision_trace.detail: Traverse the SNOMED anatomy part-of hierarchy from 'lumbar_spine' to collect all descendant anatomical nodes (e.g. patellofemoral joint, tibial plateau) that are part of the injured region.  Exercises stressing any of these nodes are candidates for exclusion.

decision_trace.name: movement_type_exclusion
decision_trace.detail: Exclude exercises that perform any of ['flexion', 'load'] at the injured joint 'lumbar_spine' based on today's pain_on check-in and the active healing phase's movement restrictions.

decision_trace.name: equipment_gate
decision_trace.detail: Remove exercises that require equipment the member does not have.

decision_trace.name: llm_structuring
decision_trace.detail: The LLM receives only the 40 safe exercises and structures them into a single WorkoutPlan — assigning sets, reps, rest, per-exercise rationale, sequencing roles, the session-level stimulus/target_adaptation/design_rationale, and the strength/conditioning/mobility stimulus distribution.
